text: The surgeon utilizes four robotic arms, each inserted through 8mm trocars, along with a 12mm utility incision to facilitate the pulmonary lobectomy procedure.
The surgeon performs dissection in an anticlockwise direction, beginning at the pulmonary ligament zone one.
The surgeon maintains the exposure for the pulmonary ligament section while performing lymphadenectomy on lymph nodes at station nine, using robotic arms introduced through trocars.
The surgeon approaches zone II, exposing the posterior hilum to facilitate dissection of the posterior side of the left pulmonary vein and the mediastinal artery.
The surgeon performs lymph node dissection at stations VII and X, maintaining the same exposure technique used previously for accessing the posterior hilum and pulmonary ligament.
The surgeon exposes the upper hilum, providing access to the superior side of the mediastinal artery and facilitating lymphadenectomy at stations ten, five, and six.
The surgeon retracts the lung towards the posterior pleural side to gain access to the anterior hilum, known as zone four.
The surgeon performs dissection around the mediastinal artery and lymph nodes at stations ten and eleven, using the exposure achieved in previous steps.
The surgeon performs a complete lymph node dissection in stations eleven and twelve, with robotic instruments controlling the artery, vein, and bronchus before sectioning.
The surgeon cuts the bronchus and proceeds with lymphadenectomy to gain access to the pulmonary artery, focusing on the dissection of lymph nodes in the surrounding area.
The surgeon applies a nail veil sheet over the sutured lung zone to provide aerostasis, ensuring the lung tissue is sealed and preventing air leaks.
The surgeon places the resected lung specimen into an angiobag for extraction following the complete resection.
The surgeon places a pleural chest tube into the thoracic cavity, ensuring proper positioning for postoperative drainage.
